title: DAVID — Charlemagne: The Emperor Who Named the Months

notes: Row 6: AI-disclosure obligation planned — verify presence at Pre-Publish.
Social = mass dissemination. Platform AI-label + community guidelines apply.
Historical Figure Gate: pre-1901 subject (died 814); 1900 ceiling and recency clear.
Gate 0 cleared for Development/Pre-Production subject to ongoing review.

sources.citation: Einhard, Vita Karoli Magni (c. 829) — Charlemagne's native Frankish speech, his Latin learning, and the Frankish month and wind names he assigned
sources.notes: Eyewitness courtier biography; ch. 25 (learning) and ch. 29 (Frankish month names) are the direct attestations

sources.citation: Notker the Stammerer, Gesta Karoli Magni (c. 887) — anecdotal Carolingian court tradition
sources.notes: Later monastic compilation; legendary in tone, used only for court-culture context

sources.citation: Royal Frankish Annals (Annales regni Francorum) — reign chronology and campaigns
sources.notes: Official Carolingian annalistic record in Latin

shots.speech_text: I am DAVID. Tonight, Charlemagne — king of the Franks, first emperor in the West since Rome, and a ruler who spoke one language and rebuilt another.
